title: Ships
description: Adds a new ship dungeon and lets illagers use boats.
body:
Ships is a structure datapack that adds illager ships into the game as a new type of dungeon, and allows illagers use boats.

<iframe width="560" height="315" src="https://www.youtube-nocookie.com/embed/ma9xrL_LGww" title="YouTube video player" frameborder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share" allowfullscreen></iframe>

# Structures
## Ships

The ships are designed to look like shipwrecks, and the ships contain pillagers, vindicators, and loot similar to shipwreck loot. Ships can spawn made out of different types of wood, and maps from the ships lead you to pillager outposts. 
![Alt text](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/M185nxi6/images/35a66b1c7b86247a055281a1309e7065eb07ef37.png 'Ship Structure')

## Fleets
Fleets are structures consisting of multiple ships. Each ship has an evoker. illagers will continuously spawn on these until you kill all the evokers. Maps from fleets lead you to woodland mansions.
![Alt text](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/M185nxi6/images/1188e24ba21a01b71e8e061b1957a54973d35a89.png 'Fleet Structure')

# Illager Boats

This datapack also allows illagers use boats. If an illager is in the water, it will get into a boat and patrol nearby ocean. If it sees an enemy, it will stop patrolling and chase it. Ships spawn with a few patrols around to make getting on board more challenging. Fleets spawn with even more patrols around them.

**The Illager boat mechanic is experimental, can be somewhat performance intensive, and might break if you have mods, plugins, or other datapacks installed!**
If you need to disable or limit Ai boats, you can do so by changing gamerules with "/function ships:gamerules"  
If you are interested in helping optimize the datapack, you can contribute [here](https://github.com/EMD0123/Ships/pulls).

![Alt text](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/M185nxi6/images/d0909c5defc9ace3c2521b579df1fcf2556ab7a6.png 'Pillager in a boat')
---


To use this datapack/mod in a modpack outside of Modrinth, you must comment on its Planet Minecraft page that you are using this datapack/mod in a modpack and include either a link to the modpack in the comment, or both its name and a link to the website it's hosted on in the comment.

Credit to Bobydon for the showcase video.
